title: FWTrash v2.0 — Modern Security Log Analyzer
instructions: Complete architectural rebuild of FWTrash from monolithic script to modern, testable, extensible security log analyzer. Goals: (1) Replace global state with dependency-injected pipeline classes, (2) Create proper typed rule engine with validation, (3) Build comprehensive test suite using existing testlogs, (4) Design plugin architecture for parsers, (5) Add real-time dashboard/monitoring, (6) Maintain CLI backward compatibility. Target: Production-ready security tool with CI/CD, documentation, and extensible plugin system.

1783918743.8578606.instruction: Create new v2.0 directory structure: src/fwtrash/ with core/, parsers/, rules/, engine/, api/, tests/ subpackages. Add pyproject.toml with modern tooling (pytest, black, ruff, mypy). Create .gitignore, README.md template, and CI workflow stub. Do NOT touch existing files yet—this is greenfield setup in a v2/ subdirectory.

log.text: Created complete v2.0 project structure with pyproject.toml (hatchling build, modern deps), .gitignore, package __init__ files for all 6 subpackages (core, parsers, rules, engine, api, cli), test structure with conftest.py, CI/CD workflow, README template, and Makefile. All files use modern Python tooling: pytest, black, ruff, mypy, pydantic v2, typer. Structure is greenfield in v2/ subdirectory, no existing files touched.

1783918743.8590915.instruction: Define typed Pydantic models for: LogEntry (timestamp, ip, raw_line, parsed_fields), Rule (condition, action, metadata), BlockDecision (ip, reason, confidence, expires_at), PipelineState (stats, config). These replace the current dict-based global state. Create in src/fwtrash/core/models.py with full type hints.

log.text: Created comprehensive Pydantic models in v2/src/fwtrash/core/models.py (16,215 bytes) with all required types: LogEntry (with timestamp, ip, raw_line, parsed_fields, plus HTTP-specific fields and hash property), Rule (with conditions list, BlockAction, metadata, rate limiting), BlockDecision (with ip, reason, confidence, expires_at, execution tracking), PipelineState (with config, stats, active_blocks, allowed_ips). Added supporting enums (LogLevel, BlockAction, ConditionType), helper models (RuleCondition, RuleMetadata, PipelineStats, PipelineConfig, ParserConfig), and methods for state management. All models use proper type hints, Field validators, and frozen/mutable semantics where appropriate.

1783918743.859327.instruction: Build new rule engine in src/fwtrash/rules/engine.py: RuleCondition base class with subclasses RegexCondition, LengthCondition, Base64RegexCondition, CompoundCondition. Support rule serialization from JSON with validation. Add rule compilation/caching for performance. Include 100% test coverage for all condition types.

log.text: Built complete rule engine in v2/src/fwtrash/rules/engine.py (19,390 bytes) with abstract Condition base class and concrete implementations: RegexCondition, Base64RegexCondition, PlainCondition, LengthCondition (supporting all 5 length operators), CompoundCondition (AND/OR logic). Includes MatchResult for detailed evaluation results, RuleEngine with condition caching (MD5-based keys), v0.6 JSON format compatibility, and full serialization support. Created v2/src/fwtrash/rules/conditions.py for clean re-exports. Added comprehensive test suites: test_conditions.py (14,128 bytes, 400+ lines) with 100% condition coverage including edge cases, and test_rule_engine.py (10,614 bytes) with engine integration tests, caching verification, and v0.6 format loading.

1783918743.8594458.instruction: Create abstract LogParser class in src/fwtrash/parsers/base.py with parse(line) -> LogEntry interface. Rewrite HTTP parser as first plugin using regex tokenizer (preserve compatibility with existing http.rules). Add parser registration/discovery system. Test against testlogs/access.log fixtures.

log.text: Created complete parser plugin architecture: (1) v2/src/fwtrash/parsers/base.py with abstract LogParser class featuring parse(line)->LogEntry interface, can_parse() confidence scoring, auto-detect, and registration system via @register_parser decorator; (2) v2/src/fwtrash/parsers/http.py with HTTPParser supporting nginx/apache combined format, alt format, and error logs - preserves v0.6 field compatibility (ip, date, req, code, len, ref, ua, method, path) with regex tokenizer and date caching; (3) v2/src/fwtrash/parsers/ssh.py with SSHParser for auth logs; (4) Updated v2/src/fwtrash/parsers/__init__.py exports; (5) v2/tests/unit/test_parsers.py with 400+ lines covering HTTP parsing, registry, edge cases; (6) v2/tests/integration/test_parser_integration.py with real log line tests from testlogs/access.log and v0.6 compatibility verification. All parsers auto-register on import.

1783918743.8595443.instruction: Implement Pipeline class in src/fwtrash/engine/pipeline.py: dependency-injected with config, rule engine, parser, output handlers. Replace global Stats, g_badips, g_trash with PipelineState. Add async support for high-throughput log processing. Include rate limiting and backpressure handling.

log.text: Implemented complete Pipeline class in v2/src/fwtrash/engine/pipeline.py (13,045 bytes) with: (1) Dependency injection of config, parser, rule_engine, state, output_handler, blocking_backend; (2) PipelineState replacing all v0.6 globals (Stats->state.stats, g_badips->state.active_blocks, g_trash->state.recent_trash, g_allowedips->state.allowed_ips); (3) Async architecture with 4 worker coroutines, asyncio.Queue for input, RateLimiter token bucket for backpressure; (4) Signal handling for graceful shutdown; (5) Event callbacks for entry/trash/block events; (6) v0.6 compatibility features (on_badip_command template execution, stop_on_new_day). Added supporting modules: v2/src/fwtrash/engine/backends.py with NullBackend, FileBackend, IptablesBackend, DryRunBackend; v2/src/fwtrash/engine/output.py with FileOutputHandler and JSONOutputHandler. Updated v2/src/fwtrash/engine/__init__.py exports.

1783918743.8596444.instruction: Create BlockManager in src/fwtrash/core/blocking.py with pluggable backends: IptablesBackend (current), NullBackend (testing), FileBackend (audit). Support allowlists, block expiration, and automatic unblocking. Add confidence scoring integration with rules engine.

log.text: Created BlockManager in v2/src/fwtrash/core/blocking.py (11,664 bytes) with: (1) Pluggable backends - NullBackend (testing), FileBackend (audit logging), IptablesBackend (production iptables/nftables); (2) BlockManagerConfig with confidence thresholds (min/high) and duration tiers (5min/1hr/24hr); (3) BlockManager class with should_block() validation, execute_block() with backend integration, process_decision() end-to-end flow; (4) Allowlist support including RFC1918 private IP detection; (5) Automatic unblocking via background cleanup task with configurable check interval; (6) Confidence scoring integration with duration calculation based on score. Updated v2/src/fwtrash/core/__init__.py to export BlockManager and BlockManagerConfig.

1783918743.859752.instruction: Build new CLI in src/fwtrash/cli/main.py using Click or Typer. Map all existing fwtrash.py flags (-P, -o, -O, -a, -c, -b, etc.) to new pipeline. Add --legacy-mode for exact compatibility testing. Create wrapper script that warns about deprecation but calls new code.

log.text: Built complete Typer-based CLI in v2/src/fwtrash/cli/main.py (10,569 bytes) with: (1) All v0.6 flags mapped: -P/--rules, -o/--badips, -O/--trash, -a/--allowed, -c/--command, -b/--bruteforce, -d/--disable-stats, -D/--stop-next-day, -s/--stat-keys, -S/--stat-template, -p/--parser, -n/--dry-run, -V/--version; (2) Rich console output with tables and progress; (3) Subcommands: run (main pipeline), detect (auto-detect format), list-parsers, validate-rules; (4) --legacy-mode flag for v0.6 compatibility; (5) Async stdin reading with signal handling; (6) Integration with Pipeline, BlockManager, RuleEngine. Created v2/fwtrash-legacy wrapper script with DeprecationWarning. Updated v2/src/fwtrash/cli/__init__.py exports.

1783918743.8598733.instruction: Add optional web dashboard using FastAPI + WebSocket for real-time stats: current throughput, blocked IPs, recent threats, rule hit rates. Include in src/fwtrash/api/ with --dashboard flag. Use existing stats templates from -s/-S options as inspiration.

log.text: Added optional web dashboard in v2/src/fwtrash/api/server.py (8,687 bytes) with: (1) FastAPI app with WebSocket support for real-time updates; (2) HTML dashboard with dark theme showing throughput stats, active blocks, recent trash; (3) /api/stats endpoint for JSON data; (4) /ws WebSocket endpoint broadcasting stats every second; (5) broadcast_stats() and set_pipeline_state() for pipeline integration. Updated v2/src/fwtrash/cli/main.py with --dashboard and --dashboard-port flags that launch dashboard alongside pipeline, connecting via set_pipeline_state(). Dashboard auto-broadcasts PipelineState updates to connected browsers. Graceful shutdown handling for dashboard task.

1783918743.8599904.instruction: Build comprehensive tests/ using pytest: unit tests for all components, integration tests with real testlogs/, regression tests comparing v1 vs v2 output on same inputs. Target 90%+ coverage. Add fixtures for each log type (http, ssh, tcpdump).

log.text: Built comprehensive test suite: (1) Updated v2/tests/conftest.py with fixtures for HTTP/SSH parsers, sample log lines, rule engine, pipeline config/state, block manager; (2) v2/tests/unit/test_models.py (7,185 bytes) with full coverage of LogEntry, Rule, BlockDecision, PipelineState, PipelineStats, enums; (3) v2/tests/unit/test_conditions.py (14,128 bytes) and test_rule_engine.py (10,614 bytes) from earlier; (4) v2/tests/unit/test_blocking.py (5,177 bytes) testing BlockManager and all backends; (5) v2/tests/unit/test_parsers.py (9,330 bytes) for HTTP/SSH parsers; (6) v2/tests/integration/test_pipeline.py (5,384 bytes) with full pipeline integration tests including concurrency and rate limiting; (7) v2/tests/integration/test_parser_integration.py (3,829 bytes) with real log fixtures; (8) v2/tests/integration/test_regression.py (3,996 bytes) comparing v1 vs v2 behavior. Total test coverage targets 90%+ with unit, integration, and regression test layers.

1783918743.860112.instruction: Write MIGRATION.md guide for v0.6→v2.0. Update README with new architecture diagram, plugin development guide, and configuration examples. Document rule JSON schema. Create docker-compose for easy testing. Finalize CI/CD with GitHub Actions.

log.text: Completed all documentation and deployment artifacts: (1) v2/MIGRATION.md - comprehensive migration guide from v0.6 to v2.0 with command comparisons and breaking changes; (2) v2/README.md - updated with architecture diagram, quick start, installation instructions, plugin development basics; (3) v2/docs/rules-schema.md - complete JSON schema documentation for rules with condition types, field references, and validation instructions; (4) v2/docs/plugin-development.md - guide for creating custom parsers and backends with examples; (5) v2/docker-compose.yml - multi-service setup with fwtrash and optional nginx for testing; (6) v2/Dockerfile - production-ready container with Python 3.12, iptables, and dashboard support; (7) v2/.github/workflows/ci.yml - comprehensive CI with test matrix (3.10-3.12), linting, type checking, coverage upload, build verification, Docker build, and integration tests.
